title: Nymphoides minor
taxonomy: Nymphoides minor is classified in the family Menyanthaceae, which is a group of aquatic plants commonly known as bogbeans or buckbean. This family is part of the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The genus Nymphoides, to which Nymphoides minor belongs, consists of approximately 40 species of aquatic plants. Nymphoides minor is a perennial, aquatic plant native to Europe, Asia, and parts of North America. It is also known as the lesser marsh-clematis or lesser water-lily.
uses: Nymphoides minor, also known as lesser duckweed, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves and roots have been employed to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. In some cultures, the plant's extracts are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. intro: Nymphoides minor, also known as lesser water-lily or small water-lily, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the family Menyanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
